ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08573Nahum (Nach) Waxman Collection of Food and Culinary Trade Cards8573Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

cmrceThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:11:24 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Nahum (Nach) Waxman Collection of Food and Culinary Trade Cards
Waxman, Nahum
8573
3.5 cubic feet. 3.5 cubic feet
circa 1850-1900. Printed Materials. This collection traces the history of food, cookery, agriculture, kitchen equipment, and more through hundreds of advertising trade cards from the second half of the 19th century. Collection material in English 9756156
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
October 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, October 2018
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
In 1983 Nach Waxman opened the New York City book store Kitchen Arts & Letters, specializing in cookbooks, gastronomy, and food books and magazines from across the world.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
This collection traces the history of food, cookery, agriculture, kitchen equipment, and more through hundreds of advertising trade cards from the second half of the 19th century.
Arrangement
Physical arrangement by donor. Intellectually arranged in alphabetical order.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Agriculture
Series II. Baking
Series III. Baking: Flours and Milling
Series IV. Baking: Rising Agents
Series V. Beverages: Nonalcoholic
Series VI. Beverages: Fermented and Distilled
Series VII. Beverages: Coffee
Series VIII. Beverages: Tea
Series IX. Candy and Other Confections
Series X. Chocolate
Series XI. Condiments
Series XII. Dairy Products
Series XIII. Extracts (Nutritional)
Series XIV. Fats and Oils
Series XV. Fish and Seafood
Series XVI. Flavorings
Series XVII. Fruit and Fruit Products
Series XVIII. Gelatin and Gelatin Products
Series XIX. Grocery Stores I
Series XX. Household: Kitchen Equipment and Tableware
Series XXI. Household: Maintenance
Series XXII. Ice Cream
Series XXIII. Meat
Series XXIV. Mince Meat and Plum Pudding
Series XXV. Medicinals
Series XXVI. Preserves, Extracts, Dried Fruits, Juices, Syrups
Series XXVII. Restaurants
Series XXVIII. Scent
Series XXIX. Scrapbook of Assorted Trade Cards
Series XXX. Seed and Nut Foods
Series XXXI. Soup
Series XXXII. Starch
Series XXXIII. Stoves
Series XXXIV. Thurbers'
Series XXXV. Vegetable People
Series XXXVI. Water and Ice
Cite As:
Nahum (Nach) Waxman Collection of Food and Culinary Trade Cards, #8573. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Trade cards. Advertising cards. Kitchens. Food. Cooking. Agriculture. Advertising.

Scope and Contents
This box contains a number of full and partial card sets for Chocolat Poulain.CondimentsSauces, Dressings, Mustards, Ketchups12Dairy ProductsMilk and Butter, Cheese, Cream, Dairy Stores12Condensed Milk12Extracts (Nutritional)Dairy-Based Extracts12Grain-Based Extracts12Imperial Granum12Mellin's12Ridge's12Wells Richardson12Other Grain-Based Extracts12Meat-Based Extracts13Liebig Extracts13Fats and OilsButter Substitutes and Shortenings14Lard14Fairbanks14Naphey's14Oils14Olive Oil14Vegetable Oils14Fish and SeafoodFish15Clams15Lobsters15Oysters and Oyster Houses15Fishmongers, Seafood Dealers15FlavoringsSpices and Flavoring Extracts16Salt17Sweeteners17Sugars17Syrups17Honey17Vinegars17Alden's17Other Vinegar Brands17Fruit and Fruit ProductsPreserves, Extracts, Dried Fruits, Juices, Syrups18Gelatin and Gelatin ProductsJell-O18Other Gelatin Brands18Grocery Stores IIndependent Grocers19Chain Grocers20Grand Union Tea Co.20Great Atlantic & Pacific Tea Co. (A & P)20Great Japan and London Tea Co.20Great London Tea Co.20Union Pacific Tea Co.20Household: Kitchen Equipment and TablewareSmall Appliances and Kitchen Tools21Stoves21Iceboxes and Refrigerators21Ironware21Tableware21Household: MaintenanceClothing Care22Laundry Soap22Clothes Wringers22Irons22Laundry Starch22Sewing Machines22Dyes22Lawn Care22Cleansing Agents22Soaps, Solvents, Polishes22Ice CreamIce Cream Shops and Parlors22Ice Cream Freezers and Other Equipment22MeatCanned Meats23Fairbank23Hammond23Libby, McNeill & Libby23St. Louis Beef23Wilson's Beef23Other Canned Meat Brands23Preserved Meat Products23Cured Hams, Sausages, Luncheon Meats23Meat Retail Shops (Butchers)24Meat Packers and Wholesalers24Libby, McNeill & Libby24St. Louis Beef24Swift & Company24Other Meat Packers24Mince Meat and Plum PuddingAtmore's24Other Mince Meat Brands24MedicinalsRemedies and Other Medicinal Products (A-J)25Ayer's25Carter's25Hood's25Horsford's25Other Remedies and Other Medicinal Products25Remedies and Other Medicinal Products (K-Z)26Lydia Pinkham's26Remedies and Other Medicinal Products26Hair Care26Dental Care26Preserves, Extracts, Dried Fruits, Juices, SyrupsPickles and Relishes27Sweet Conserves27
Scope and Contents
For additional fruit preserves, see Box 18.Canned Products and Canning27RestaurantsRestaurants, Lunch Rooms, Dining Rooms, Coffee Rooms, Ladies' Lunch Rooms, Cafes, Coffee Saloons, Pavilions, Dining Parlors, Dining and Oyster Saloons, and Other Dining Establishments27Scent28Scrapbook of Assorted Trade Cards37Seed and Nut Foods28Coconuts28Peanuts and Tree Nuts28Soup28Starch
Scope and Contents
From donor: This category unavoidably merges alimentary starches, used in cooking, and laundry starches, used in ironing. Nearly ever manufacturer produced both of these products, and much of their advertising promotes brand rather than one or the other type of starch.Alimentary and Laundry Starch28Stoves
Scope and Contents
From donor: This category merges two pieces of equipment that were distinguished in the 19th and early 20th centuries -- stoves, primarily for heating, and ranges, used primarily for cooking.Wood and Coal Stoves (A-J)29Wood and Coal Stoves (K-Z)30Oil and Gas Stoves30Stove Accessories30Stove Linings30Stove Polishes30Stove Dealers30Thurbers'31
Scope and Contents
From donor: This is a brand name, the only one given its own place in this list of categories. Thurbers', one of the leading users of handsomely designed trade cards, was the brand of H. K. & F. B. Thurber & Co., founded in 1875 and known after 1884 as Thurber, Whyland, & Co. A world-known company, with branches in London and Bordeaux, it produced and distributed a wide variety of goods, ranging from canned fruits, vegetables, and meat to soaps, coffee, dairy goods, and flavoring extracts.Vegetable People31
Scope and Contents
From donor: A special design category, cutting across a wide variety of subjects. Popular from the early days of American trade cards, these caricatures employ the images of vegetables or fruit to which are added arms, legs, and facial features to portray humanlike figures. They remain a favorite of collections.Water and Ice31 ======= AgricultureFarming Equipment1General1Mowers and Harvesters1Binders1Plows1Furnaces and Boilers1Nails1Fencing1Other Farm Equipment1Fertilizers1Animal Seed1Seed1BakingGrains2Corn2Oats2Farinose, Avena2Wheat2Grain-Based Breakfast Foods2Breakfast Cereals2Pancakes and Waffles2Baking: Flours and MillingWheat Flour3Buckwheat Flour3Flour Dealers3Baking: Rising AgentsBaking Soda and Baking Powder (A-H)4Baking Soda and Baking Powder (I-Z)5Yeast6Fleischmann6Other Yeast Brands6Bakeries7Bakery Products7Bread7Cakes7Biscuits (Cookies and Crackers)7Beverages: NonalcoholicSodas, Tonics, Phosphates8Beverages: Fermented and DistilledWhiskies, Brandies, Cordials, Allied Beverages8Beer, Ale, Cider8Wines,8Still Wines8Sparkling Wines, Champagne8Alcoholic Beverage Sellers8Bars, Sampling Rooms, Taverns, Alehouses, and Other Drinking Establishments8Retail and Wholesale Dealers8Beverages: CoffeeCoffee9Coffee Brands9Coffee and Tea Dealers9Coffee Substitutes9Arbuckle/Ariosa Coffee10Beverages: TeaTea Brands11Tea Houses and Tea Stores11Candy and Other ConfectionsCandy and Confectionary Shops32Chewing Gum32ChocolateChocolate and Chocolate Producers33Cocoa33Stollwerck's Group 1-17534Stollwerck's Group 176-49635Poulain Chocolate Sets36

For additional fruit preserves, see Box 18.Canned Products and Canning27RestaurantsRestaurants, Lunch Rooms, Dining Rooms, Coffee Rooms, Ladies' Lunch Rooms, Cafes, Coffee Saloons, Pavilions, Dining Parlors, Dining and Oyster Saloons, and Other Dining Establishments27Scent28Scrapbook of Assorted Trade Cards37Seed and Nut Foods28Coconuts28Peanuts and Tree Nuts28Soup28Starch

Nearly ever manufacturer produced both of these products, and much of their advertising promotes brand rather than one or the other type of starch.Alimentary and Laundry Starch28Stoves

From donor: This category merges two pieces of equipment that were distinguished in the 19th and early 20th centuries -- stoves, primarily for heating, and ranges, used primarily for cooking.Wood and Coal Stoves (A-J)29Wood and Coal Stoves (K-Z)30Oil and Gas Stoves30Stove Accessories30Stove Linings30Stove Polishes30Stove Dealers30Thurbers'31
Scope and Contents
From donor: This is a brand name, the only one given its own place in this list of categories. Thurbers', one of the leading users of handsomely designed trade cards, was the brand of H. K. & F. B. Thurber & Co., founded in 1875 and known after 1884 as Thurber, Whyland, & Co. A world-known company, with branches in London and Bordeaux, it produced and distributed a wide variety of goods, ranging from canned fruits, vegetables, and meat to soaps, coffee, dairy goods, and flavoring extracts.Vegetable People31
Scope and Contents
From donor: A special design category, cutting across a wide variety of subjects. Popular from the early days of American trade cards, these caricatures employ the images of vegetables or fruit to which are added arms, legs, and facial features to portray humanlike figures.
